point_reviews.reason: The document states that COVID-19 is caused by SARS-CoV-2, while the common cold and flu are caused by different viruses.
The document does not support that COVID-19 symptoms can range from mild to severe in the same way as flu and cold symptoms, as it emphasizes the need for testing to confirm COVID-19.
The document mentions that COVID-19 can lead to serious medical complications, including multisystem inflammatory syndrome.
The document lists loss of taste or smell as a symptom of COVID-19, which is not common in cold or flu cases.
The document states that COVID-19 can cause severe illness in people with pre-existing conditions, unlike colds and flu.
The document confirms that COVID-19 can lead to serious complications and death, unlike the common cold or flu.
The document states that SARS-CoV-2 is genetically distinct from the viruses that cause the common cold and flu.
COVID-19 can lead to serious complications and death, unlike the common cold or flu, which are generally less severe.
COVID-19 symptoms can include loss of taste or smell, which is not typical for the common cold or flu.
The virus causing COVID-19, SARS-CoV-2, is genetically distinct from the viruses that cause the common cold and flu.
COVID-19 spreads primarily through respiratory droplets, similar to the flu, but can also spread from asymptomatic individuals.
COVID-19 can cause severe illness in people with pre-existing conditions, while colds and flu generally have milder outcomes.
